req.title: External ACH transfer (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A customer shall transfer funds to an external ACH-linked account up to $10,000 per transfer; transfers above this shall require step-up verification. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines five distinct test-worthy behaviours: (1) a valid ACH transfer within the $10,000 limit queues successfully (EP positive partition); (2) a transfer exceeding $10,000 without step-up is rejected with 422 (NEG / DT); (3) the exact $10,000 boundary is accepted (BVA upper-on-boundary); (4) $10,001 is rejected – the first value above the boundary (BVA off-boundary); (5) a duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key returns the original response without creating a second transfer (STC idempotency state). Insufficient-funds (409) adds a sixth negative DT cell. All tests are self-contained via fixtures that create and teardown isolated test accounts, and each uses a unique UUID idempotency key so repeated pytest runs stay deterministic.

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_valid_amount_queued
preconditions: Customer account exists with sufficient balance (>= $500)
ACH link is verified for the destination account
actions: Generate a unique Idempotency-Key UUID
POST /transfers/ach with amount=500.00, valid ach_link_id, and Idempotency-Key header
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-079: transfers up to $10,000 shall be accepted and ACH batch queued
R-FIN-079: confirmation sent on successful submission
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_ach_transfer_valid_amount_queued(api_client, verified_ach_customer):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "ach_link_id": verified_ach_customer["ach_link_id"],
        "amount": "500.00",
        "currency": "USD",
        "memo": "EP positive test"
    }
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {verified_ach_customer['token']}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 202, f"Expected 202, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transfer_id" in body, "Missing transfer_id in response"
    assert body.get("status") == "queued", f"Expected status=queued, got {body.get('status')}"
    assert "confirmation_reference" in body, "Missing confirmation_reference"

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_above_limit_without_stepup_rejected
preconditions: Customer account exists with sufficient balance (>= $15,000)
actions: Generate a unique Idempotency-Key UUID
POST /transfers/ach with amount=10001.00 and NO step-up header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response error code indicates step-up required
No transfer_id created in the system
trace: R-FIN-079: transfers above $10,000 require step-up verification
R-FIN-079: error pathway 422 when amount > 10000 without step-up
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_ach_transfer_above_limit_without_stepup_rejected(api_client, verified_ach_customer):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "ach_link_id": verified_ach_customer["ach_link_id"],
        "amount": "10001.00",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {verified_ach_customer['token']}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_code = body.get("error", {}).get("code", "")
    assert "step_up" in error_code.lower() or "limit" in error_code.lower(), \
        f"Unexpected error code: {error_code}"
    assert "transfer_id" not in body, "transfer_id must not be present on rejection"

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_exact_limit_boundary_accepted
preconditions: Customer account exists with sufficient balance (>= $10,000)
No step-up token required (amount is exactly at the limit)
actions: Generate a unique Idempotency-Key UUID
POST /transfers/ach with amount=10000.00 (exact boundary)
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-079: transfers UP TO $10,000 shall be accepted
BVA: on-boundary value 10000.00 must be in the valid partition
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_ach_transfer_exact_limit_boundary_accepted(api_client, verified_ach_customer):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "ach_link_id": verified_ach_customer["ach_link_id"],
        "amount": "10000.00",
        "currency": "USD",
        "memo": "BVA on-boundary test"
    }
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {verified_ach_customer['token']}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 202, f"Expected 202 at boundary, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transfer_id" in body
    assert body.get("status") == "queued"

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_one_cent_above_limit_rejected
preconditions: Customer account exists with sufficient balance (>= $10,001)
actions: Generate a unique Idempotency-Key UUID
POST /transfers/ach with amount=10000.01 (one cent above boundary)
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response error references the transfer limit or step-up requirement
trace: R-FIN-079: amount > 10000 without step-up must return 422
BVA: off-boundary value 10000.01 must be in the invalid partition
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_ach_transfer_one_cent_above_limit_rejected(api_client, verified_ach_customer):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "ach_link_id": verified_ach_customer["ach_link_id"],
        "amount": "10000.01",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {verified_ach_customer['token']}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422 off-boundary, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body, "Response must contain error details"

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_idempotent_duplicate_returns_original
preconditions: Customer account exists with sufficient balance (>= $200)
A specific Idempotency-Key has not been used before this test run
actions: Generate a single Idempotency-Key UUID shared across both calls
POST /transfers/ach (first submission) with amount=200.00 and the key
Capture first response transfer_id and status
POST /transfers/ach (duplicate submission) with identical payload and same key
Capture second response and compare to first
expected: First call returns HTTP 202 with transfer_id and status=queued
Second call returns HTTP 200 or 202 (idempotent replay)
Second call transfer_id equals first call transfer_id
Second call status equals first call status
No duplicate transfer created in the system
trace: R-FIN-079: duplicate submission with same Idempotency-Key must return original result
STC: state transitions from initial -> queued must not repeat on replay
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_ach_transfer_idempotent_duplicate_returns_original(api_client, verified_ach_customer):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "ach_link_id": verified_ach_customer["ach_link_id"],
        "amount": "200.00",
        "currency": "USD",
        "memo": "STC idempotency test"
    }
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {verified_ach_customer['token']}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key
    }
    resp1 = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp1.status_code == 202, f"First call failed: {resp1.status_code} {resp1.text}"
    body1 = resp1.json()
    assert "transfer_id" in body1

    resp2 = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp2.status_code in (200, 202), f"Duplicate call unexpected status: {resp2.status_code}"
    body2 = resp2.json()
    assert body2.get("transfer_id") == body1["transfer_id"], \
        "Idempotency violation: duplicate returned different transfer_id"
    assert body2.get("status") == body1.get("status"), \
        "Idempotency violation: status differs on replay"

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_insufficient_funds_returns_409
preconditions: Customer account exists with balance LESS THAN the transfer amount (e.g. balance=$10.00)
actions: Generate a unique Idempotency-Key UUID
POST /transfers/ach with amount=500.00 (exceeds available balance)
Capture HTTP response status and error body
trace: R-FIN-079: error pathway 409 on insufficient funds
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_ach_transfer_insufficient_funds_returns_409(api_client, low_balance_ach_customer):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "ach_link_id": low_balance_ach_customer["ach_link_id"],
        "amount": "500.00",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {low_balance_ach_customer['token']}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 409, f"Expected 409, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_code = body.get("error", {}).get("code", "")
    assert "insufficient" in error_code.lower() or "funds" in error_code.lower(), \
        f"Unexpected error code: {error_code}"
    assert "transfer_id" not in body
